name: Bronchogen
subtitle: AEDL Tetrapeptide | Bronchial Bioregulator

molecular.halfLife: Minutes (short peptide); effects persist via epigenetic changes

overview: Bronchogen is a Khavinson bioregulator tetrapeptide (AEDL) with primary effects on the bronchopulmonary system. Developed at Russia's St. Petersburg Institute of Bioregulation and Gerontology by Professor Vladimir Khavinson, it targets bronchial tissue and supports respiratory function. Like other Khavinson peptides, Bronchogen penetrates cell nuclei to influence gene expression related to respiratory tissue maintenance and repair.
mechanism: Bronchogen works through epigenetic regulation by penetrating cell and nuclear membranes to interact with DNA and modulate gene expression in bronchial tissue. It regulates protein synthesis in bronchopulmonary cells, supporting maintenance and repair of respiratory epithelium. The tetrapeptide structure allows efficient cellular uptake and tissue-specific targeting to bronchial structures.

items.name: Bronchial Function
items.description: Supports bronchial tissue through gene expression regulation.

items.name: Pulmonary Health
items.description: Regulates protein synthesis in lung tissue.

items.name: Respiratory Aging
items.description: Addresses age-related changes in bronchial tissue.

items.name: Gene Expression
items.description: Modulates gene expression in respiratory cells.

deliveryMethods.overview: Available in capsule form for oral administration. Short peptides can be absorbed orally and reach target tissues. Typical protocol involves 10-20 day cycles.
Injectable form available for direct administration.

interactions.notes: Often combined in comprehensive anti-aging Khavinson protocols.
Both have immune-modulating properties; different tissue targets.
Part of Khavinson bioregulator family; targets different tissue.

timeline.effects: Effects persist due to epigenetic changes
